title: ESPN reporter explains why June 1st could be a big day for teams looking to trade for A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Trading A.J. Brown before June 1 would cost the Eagles $43.5 million in dead cap, post-June 1, it drops to $16.353 million.
The New England Patriots are interested in A.J. Brown, with their current receiving core mostly under 25 and lacking 1,000-yard seasons.
The Eagles' asking price for Brown is similar to a 'Quinnen Williams-type deal', involving high draft picks and possibly a player.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Allyson Bogdanski, 45, from Medford, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother's battle with breast cancer.
Her mother, a patient at Dana-Farber, is a two-time breast cancer survivor owing her success to the hospital's care.
Bogdanski views the marathon as a tribute to her mom's courage and the support from Dana-Farber's medical team.

title: GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: GoFundMe for Officer Nicholas O'Malley, charged with manslaughter, raises over $407,000.
O'Malley was suspended after the fatal shooting of carjacking suspect Stephenson King in Roxbury.
Boston city councilors and police union express support, stressing caution in making judgments.

title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are considering new legislation to regulate AI in political advertising to prevent misleading voters.
State representative Marc Lombardo used an AI-generated attack ad against Daniel Darris-O'Connor in a Billerica House seat race.
Two bills, H.5093 and H.5094, propose restrictions and disclosure requirements for AI-generated political content.

title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, from Ashburnham, died after his car fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster, according to police.
A GoFundMe campaign, created by a relative, has raised over $70,000 to support Teixeira's family with funeral expenses and other costs.
The fundraiser aims to assist the family as they cope with the loss and take time off work to grieve.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz gave a memorable speech at Fenway Park after the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings.
Ortiz's speech included a defiant declaration: "This is our [expletive] city," which resonated deeply with Boston residents.
Ortiz's words were delivered during a Red Sox pregame ceremony five days after the tragic event.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Holy Cross student Dominick Tucci, 20, is missing after a canoe capsized on Lake Hayward, East Haddam, Connecticut.
Search efforts involving water crews and aerial surveillance were suspended Sunday evening, set to resume Monday morning.
Holy Cross offers counseling services to students as they remain in contact with police during the ongoing search.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a youth mentor and founder of Big Business Network, was fatally shot on Greenwood Street, Dorchester, in 2019.
Price's mother, Carol, struggles with his unsolved murder, urging anyone with information to come forward to police.
The Boston Police Department maintains Price's case as open and active, seeking tips to solve the murder.

title: The basement of this $4.7m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: 1 Haywood Farm, a Lynnfield home listed for $4.695 million, features a basement with a bar, screening room, and playroom.
The property is known for elaborate holiday decorations that draw thousands of visitors each Halloween.
The home includes a wine tasting room, spa-grade sauna, outdoor grilling station, and stone patio with fire pit.

title: Patriots reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots plan to release quarterback Joshua Dobbs, who signed a two-year deal in 2025.
Dobbs, 31, played four games last season, completing 7 of 10 passes for 65 yards.
Tommy DeVito's return to the Patriots on a two-year deal may influence the decision to release Dobbs.
